title: poa chathamica
intro: Poa chathamica, also known as Chatham grass, is a species of grass native to New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Poa chathamica include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to support digestive health. Poa chathamica can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its extracts have also been studied for their potential use in the development of new medicines.
